caption.text: Developers are not UX designers
and UX designers are not developers.
I'm a bit tired of seeing SaaS founders think they just
need a developer.
that everything boils down to "if it works technically
then it will work for the user".
Except no.
A developer is there to make the product work solidly and securely,
and a UX designer is there to ensure it is understood,
used and adopted. These are two different professions,
two different ways of thinking and two different responsibilities.
The problem
is that developers are often asked to do a job that is not theirs.
And conversely,
a UX designer is not there to develop an app.
So when a SaaS is designed solely from a technical angle,
the problem is always the same:
the product works, but nobody really uses it.
A good SaaS doesn't come from one or the other,
but from good collaboration.
When the developer tells you "technically here's what's possible", and the UX designer
responds: "here's how we make it simple".
One without the other
results in a lopsided product. and all successful SaaSs have understood this,
UX design isn't about making it look pretty.
it's about structuring decisions,
reducing mental load and guiding users.
So if your SaaS is difficult to use,
it's not necessarily a developer problem.
It might be that the UX wasn't really treated as a real subject.
